title: deverra tortuosa
intro: Deverra tortuosa, commonly known as New Zealand thorny tree or Deverra, is a plant species that belongs to the family Apiaceae. This plant is known for its traditional medicinal uses, which include providing relief from various health issues. The health benefits of Deverra tortuosa include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Deverra tortuosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects.
benefits: Deverra tortuosa has many benefits, such as its effectiveness in treating various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and even certain types of cancer. Its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties can also help alleviate symptoms of digestive problems, reduce fever, and boost the immune system. Additionally, the plant's antioxidant properties can improve overall health by protecting against cell damage, promoting healthy aging, and even supporting cognitive function. By incorporating Deverra tortuosa into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved health, increased energy levels, and enhanced overall well-being, leading to a more productive and fulfilling life.
intro_study: According to "Plants (Basel, Switzerland)", Deverra tortuosa benefits include relieving stomach pains, against intestinal parasites, and for the regulation of menstruation.
constituents: Deverra tortuosa has many active constituents, such as alkaloids, flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which possess various medicinal properties. These constituents include devetrioside, devilline, and devillidine, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant activities, thereby reducing pain and inflammation in individuals suffering from arthritis and other conditions. The plant's flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, have also been found to have anti-cancer properties, protecting against cell damage and promoting overall health. By utilizing the medicinal constituents of Deverra tortuosa, individuals can experience improved cardiovascular health, enhanced immune function, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, ultimately leading to a better quality of life.
parts: Deverra tortuosa has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, which contain sesquiterpenoid compounds like devaric acid, as well as alkaloids like devaricine and devetropine that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of this plant are rich in flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The essential oil extracted from the flowers and leaves of Deverra tortuosa contains sesquiterpenes and monoterpenes, which have been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the plant's bark is a source of tannins and other polyphenolic compounds, which may contribute to its traditional use as a treatment for various ailments.
side_effects: Deverra tortuosa can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems. Overconsumption of the plant's extracts can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's oils has been linked to liver and kidney damage, as well as respiratory issues like asthma and bronchitis. In severe cases, improper use of Deverra tortuosa can even lead to seizures and coma.
